message:
feat(vc_alert): add default alert rules and expand test coverage

**New Default Alert Rules:**
- dcg-critical-block: Fires on critical severity command blocks
- agent-stuck: Warns when no agent activity for 10 minutes
- rch-queue-pressure: Warns on remote compilation queue buildup
- memory-critical: Critical alert at 95%+ memory usage

**E2E Test Improvements:**
- Better VC_LAST_OUTPUT capture in test assertions
- Consistent run_vc_or_skip usage across collector tests
- Improved error handling and skip conditions

These rules provide baseline monitoring out of the box:
- DCG integration surfaces dangerous command attempts
- Agent activity monitoring catches stuck processes
- RCH queue depth prevents compilation bottlenecks
- Memory alerts catch resource exhaustion early

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-new.4","title":"Implement daily/weekly digest reports","description":"## Background
Operators need a compact daily/weekly digest that summarizes fleet health, alerts, usage, and progress without opening the UI. This is a low‑friction “ops heartbeat.”

## Goals
- Generate Markdown + JSON digests for daily/weekly windows.
- Optionally deliver via MCP Agent Mail or webhook (reuse alert delivery channels where possible).
- Include health, alerts, usage, cost, and beads throughput.

## Implementation
### Digest Content
- Top alerts + incident counts.
- Health score trends (overall + top 3 machines).
- Usage & rate‑limit forecast summary.
- Beads throughput + top blockers (bv snapshot).
- Notable events (dcg spikes, rano anomalies, pt findings).

### CLI/Automation
- `vc report --window 24h --format md|json`.
- Schedule via cron/systemd or vc daemon tick.

### Tables
- `digest_reports(report_id, window, generated_at, summary_json, markdown)`.

## Risks
- Over‑verbose digests; must stay concise.
- Requires reliable data freshness indicators (bd-new.1).

## Success Criteria
- Digest is <1 page and highlights actionable items.
- Reports are reproducible for the same window.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for aggregation logic.
- Golden‑file tests for Markdown rendering.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_digest_reports.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:58.471678201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:16.880663734Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1h6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:58.471678201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2br","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:16.094989777Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2mv.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:58.471678201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2xm.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:58.471678201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.4","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:16.880196994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.4","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:58.471678201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.4","depends_on_id":"bd-new.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:40:18.889556257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.4","depends_on_id":"bd-new.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:40:21.888263503Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.4","depends_on_id":"bd-rf7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:58.471678201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-new.5","title":"Implement vc-node push agent (store‑and‑forward)","description":"## Background
Pull‑only works for small fleets, but as machine count grows it becomes expensive and brittle. A tiny vc‑node enables store‑and‑forward collection when remotes are offline and reduces SSH overhead.

## Goals
- Build a lightweight vc‑node binary that runs locally on remote machines.
- Collect locally, buffer artifacts, and push snapshots to vc‑hub on interval.
- Support offline backfill (store‑and‑forward) with deduplication.

## Implementation
### vc‑node
- Minimal config: hub URL/SSH target, machine_id, enabled collectors.
- Local spool directory for compressed JSONL/Parquet batches.
- Signed manifest per batch (hash + size + schema version).

### Hub Ingest
- Add `vc ingest --from <bundle>` to import snapshots.
- Deduplicate using (machine_id, collector, cursor, payload_hash).
- Record ingest audit events.

### Transfer Modes
- SSH push (scp/rsync) for MVP.
- Optional HTTPS upload later.

## Risks
- Artifact size growth if network is down; require max spool size.
- Duplicate ingestion if dedupe keys are weak.

## Success Criteria
- Remote machine can collect without continuous SSH polling.
- Backfilled bundles ingest cleanly with no duplicates.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for manifest hashing + dedupe keys.
- Integration tests for bundle ingest with synthetic fixtures.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/remote/test_vc_node_push.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:18.627602821Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:17.770662983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:18.627128377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3nb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3nb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3nb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-new.6","title":"Implement Prometheus exporter for vc metrics","description":"## Background
vc should integrate with existing observability stacks. A Prometheus exporter lets you build Grafana dashboards and alerts without custom tooling.

## Goals
- Expose `/metrics` endpoint with vc internal metrics and key fleet KPIs.
- Include collector freshness, alert counts, and health scores.
- Keep labels stable and documented.
- Provide a starter Grafana dashboard JSON.

## Implementation
### Metrics
- `vc_collector_freshness_seconds{machine,collector}`
- `vc_collector_success_total{machine,collector}`
- `vc_alerts_open_total{severity}`
- `vc_health_score{machine}`
- `vc_poll_duration_seconds{machine}`

### Server
- Add metrics router to vc_web or standalone metrics server.
- Use `prometheus` crate + `hyper` or axum.

### Grafana
- Ship a `docs/grafana/vc_overview.json` template referencing these metrics.

## Risks
- High-cardinality labels; must cap machine/collector label sets.

## Success Criteria
- Metrics scrape works locally and remotely.
- Grafana dashboard can be built from exposed metrics.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for metric registration + label cardinality limits.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/web/test_prometheus_export.sh` with scrape verification + JSON summary.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:17.991935727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:20.384261670Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.6","depends_on_id":"bd-15a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:17.991935727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1h6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:17.991935727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2xm.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:19.458007829Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.6","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:20.383549438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3e2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:17.991935727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.6","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:17.991935727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.6","depends_on_id":"bd-new.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:40:27.764057727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-new.7","title":"Implement GitHub issues/PR collector + correlation","description":"## Background
Repo status is more useful when correlated with issues/PRs and repo activity. This integrates GitHub metadata to show “what’s blocked” and “what’s waiting.”

## Goals
- Collect issues/PR counts, states, and labels per repo.
- Correlate with ru repo_id for dashboards and alerts.
- Cache results to avoid API rate limits.

## Implementation
### Integration
- Use `gh` CLI or GitHub REST API with token from config.
- Support org-wide + per-repo queries.
- Cache ETag/If‑Modified‑Since to reduce API calls.

### Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE gh_repo_issue_pr_snapshot(
    repo_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    open_issues INTEGER,
    open_prs INTEGER,
    triage_json TEXT,
    label_breakdown_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (repo_id, collected_at)
);
```

## Risks
- API throttling; must respect rate limits.
- Repo mapping inconsistencies (path vs URL).

## Success Criteria
- Repo dashboard shows issue/PR counts and top labels.
- Collector respects GitHub rate limits.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for mapping repo_id to GH repo.
- Mock API tests for rate-limit handling.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/collectors/test_github_collect.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:27.672542286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:22.376051994Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:21.363975126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:27.672542286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:27.672542286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.7","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:22.375529078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.7","depends_on_id":"bd-33h.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:27.672542286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.7","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:27.672542286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-new.8","title":"Implement cost attribution analytics","description":"## Background
We track usage (caut/caam) and session volume (cass) but lack cost attribution per repo/agent. This is crucial for optimizing spend and planning.

## Goals
- Attribute cost and usage to repo, machine, and agent type.
- Estimate cost per completed bead/issue where possible.
- Surface top cost drivers in TUI/web/robot.
- Emit confidence scores for attribution quality.

## Implementation
### Inputs
- caut usage snapshots, caam profiles, cass session stats, ru repo mappings.
- Map sessions to repo via cass metadata + repo paths.
- Confidence score derived from match quality (path match, session metadata, repo activity window).

### Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE cost_attribution_snapshot(
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    repo_id TEXT,
    machine_id TEXT,
    agent_type TEXT,
    provider TEXT,
    estimated_cost_usd REAL,
    tokens_used BIGINT,
    sessions_count INTEGER,
    confidence REAL,
    raw_json TEXT
);
```

### Output
- “Top cost drivers” widget.
- `vc robot cost --json` summary.

## Risks
- Imperfect mapping of sessions to repos; document confidence score.

## Success Criteria
- Cost summaries match manual sanity checks within ±10%.
- Top 5 cost drivers visible in UI.
- Low-confidence rows are flagged.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for mapping + cost estimation.
- Fixture‑based tests for cross‑tool joins.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_cost_attribution.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:23.319092129Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2mv.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2mv.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2mv.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:23.318314434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-33h.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-new.9","title":"Implement schema registry + robot/API docs generator","description":"## Background
Robot outputs and API schemas must remain stable for agents. A formal schema registry + doc generator prevents contract drift and makes integration safe.

## Goals
- Maintain JSON Schema definitions for robot outputs, config, and web API responses.
- Generate human‑readable docs + examples automatically.
- Validate outputs in tests (schema compliance).
- Detect schema changes in CI via diff checks.

## Implementation
### Schema Registry
- Store schema files under `docs/schemas/` with versioning.
- Add `vc robot-docs schemas|examples` to render docs.
- Include config schema for `vc.toml` validation.

### Validation
- Test helper to validate JSON against schema in unit tests and E2E.
- Fail fast if schema version mismatches.
- CI step to diff generated schemas vs committed ones.

## Risks
- Schema maintenance overhead; automate generation where possible.

## Success Criteria
- Robot outputs pass schema validation in CI.
- Docs list all schemas with examples.
- Schema diffs are explicit and reviewed.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for schema validation helpers.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_robot_schemas.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:46.451400553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:24.353125511Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:46.451400553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:46.451400553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.9","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:24.352303753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3e2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:46.451400553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.9","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:46.451400553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-rf7","title":"Implement bv + br (Beads) collector","description":"Implement collector for bv (Beads Viewer) and br (Beads Rust) - task tracking and productivity metrics.

INTEGRATION METHOD:
- bv --robot-triage for mega-command JSON output
- br ready --json for actionable work
- br sync --flush-only to export JSONL, then read .beads/issues.jsonl
- Use CLI Snapshot pattern

DUCKDB TABLES:
CREATE TABLE beads_triage_snapshots(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    repo_id TEXT,
    quick_ref_json TEXT,
    recommendations_json TEXT,
    project_health_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, repo_id)
);

CREATE TABLE beads_issues(
    repo_id TEXT,
    issue_id TEXT,
    status TEXT,
    priority TEXT,
    type TEXT,
    title TEXT,
    labels_json TEXT,
    deps_json TEXT,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (repo_id, issue_id)
);

CREATE TABLE beads_graph_metrics(
    repo_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    pagerank_json TEXT,
    betweenness_json TEXT,
    critical_path_json TEXT,
    cycles_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (repo_id, collected_at)
);

COLLECTOR IMPLEMENTATION:
- Implement BeadsCollector with Collector trait
- Run bv --robot-triage in each repo with .beads directory
- Parse triage output for quick_ref, recommendations, health
- Store graph metrics (pagerank, betweenness, critical path)
- Handle missing bv/br binaries gracefully

REPO DISCOVERY:
- Get repo list from ru collector data
- Check each repo for .beads directory
- Only collect from repos with beads initialized

UI VALUE:
- What to work on next across all projects
- Project health at a glance
- Bottleneck identification
- Dependency visualization

TESTS:
- Create test .beads databases
- Mock bv --robot-triage output
- Verify issue synchronization

## E2E & Logging
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/collectors/test_<collector>.sh` (see bd-30z).
- Emit structured logs: collector, machine_id, duration_ms, rows_inserted, stderr.
- Write JSON summary artifact to `tests/logs/`.
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:48:13.569904049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:01:14.097737218Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rf7","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:00:10.806701196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rf7","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:53:19.316979612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rf7","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:53:17.414649418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rf7","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T20:37:34.354059880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rf7","depends_on_id":"bd-33h","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:55:37.448394277Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rf7","depends_on_id":"bd-33h.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:53:21.077661621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -58,10 +58,12 @@ row_cmd=$(sqlite3 "$DB_PATH" "SELECT cmd FROM events WHERE id = 1;")
 assert_contains "$row_cmd" "git reset --hard" "event command should match"
 
 # Test 4: Invoke vc collect for dcg (best-effort)
-collect_output=$(run_vc_or_skip collect --collector dcg 2>&1) || {
+run_vc_or_skip collect --collector dcg 2>&1 || {
+    collect_output="$VC_LAST_OUTPUT"
     test_warn "dcg collector invocation returned non-zero"
